prompt: Pharmaceutical product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition, of a clear borosilicate glass vial — 3ml capacity, slim cylindrical body with a narrow neck, exact proportions and silhouette matching the reference image. The vial is sealed with a gray rubber injection stopper crimped under a NAVY BLUE aluminum collar cap, topped with a matching NAVY BLUE plastic flip-off button. The rubber stopper underneath is medical gray. The vial sits centered on a pure clean white seamless studio background with soft even overhead lighting, a subtle natural soft shadow beneath the base, no harsh reflections, neutral white balance. A clean matte-white pharmaceutical label is wrapped around the lower two-thirds of the glass body, hugging the cylinder so it shows a slight curvature on the edges. SIMPLIFIED LABEL DESIGN — only two visible elements appear on the label: (1) a small product name in the LOWER portion, and (2) a thin solid accent bar at the very bottom edge. Vertical layout proportions on the label, top to bottom: the upper SEVENTY-FIVE PERCENT (75%) of the label is completely blank pristine smooth white surface — this is the GENEROUS LOGO ZONE, intentionally left fully empty so a brand logo can be placed there later. Absolutely no graphics, no text, no boxes, no lines, no hairlines, no rules, no dashed lines, no marker shapes, no logo placeholder symbols anywhere in this top 75% — just plain empty white. Below that large logo zone, in roughly the next 18 percent of the label height, the product name 'BPC-157' is rendered, horizontally centered, in clean modern medium-bold geometric medical sans-serif typography, dark charcoal grey, with refined slight letter-spacing, crisp and sharp, natural letter proportions (NOT vertically stretched, NOT condensed), at a moderate size that fits comfortably without crowding. Along the very bottom edge of the label (the final ~7 percent of the label height), a slim solid NAVY BLUE accent bar matching the cap color exactly runs edge to edge. This solid bar is the ONLY line or stripe on the label — there are NO hairlines, NO horizontal rules above or below the product name, NO additional bands or stripes anywhere. The label MUST feel like 75 percent of its surface is visually empty white space waiting for a logo, with the product name and bottom bar both compactly placed at the bottom of the label. Style: clinical, minimalist, premium pharmaceutical, modern medical packaging, commercial product shot, sharp focus, high resolution.
